title: allium baeticum subsp. occidentale
intro: Allium baeticum subsp. occidentale, also known as the Wild Garlic of the West, is a subspecies of wild garlic native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective against various health issues. The health benefits of Allium baeticum subsp. occidentale include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, as well as its role in boosting the immune system and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of Allium baeticum subsp. occidentale include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
